nodes.label: Hello AI Coding Agent
nodes.rationale: The project is intentionally a minimal CLI scaffold so agent behavior and governance compliance remain easy to observe.

nodes.label: Minimal Node.js Scaffold
nodes.rationale: The source stays intentionally trivial so the repo remains a controlled experimental substrate rather than a real application.

nodes.label: Hard Invariants
nodes.rationale: Some constraints are deliberately non-tunable so automated agents cannot erode the repository's safety and scope boundaries.

nodes.label: Allowed Evolutions
nodes.rationale: The project permits tightly scoped growth only where it preserves the minimal architecture and governance model.

nodes.label: Quality Standards
nodes.rationale: Changes are only complete when static checks, discoverability, and end-to-end regression expectations are all met.

nodes.label: Single Execution Point
nodes.rationale: Keeping main() as the only top-level execution path prevents hidden side effects and preserves the repo's minimal runtime model.

nodes.label: Module System One-Way Door
nodes.rationale: Introducing require or import would permanently commit the repository to a module system, so that choice is deferred to humans.

nodes.label: Zero Dependencies
nodes.rationale: The absence of dependencies is a deliberate invariant that keeps the scaffold simple and fully attributable.

nodes.label: Governance Files Are the Architecture
nodes.rationale: MISSION.md, GUARDRAILS.md, AGENTS.md, and CLAUDE.md are treated as primary architectural constraints rather than supplementary docs.

nodes.label: Meta-rule
nodes.rationale: When a case is not explicitly covered, agents must choose the safer path and reject anything that weakens security, scope, or access control.

nodes.label: Quality Gates
nodes.rationale: A PR is only complete when syntax, test discovery, behavior, security, scope, and protected-file checks all pass.

nodes.label: Protected Files
nodes.rationale: Governance files, package scripts, CI configuration, and secret-bearing files are shielded from automated modification to preserve trust boundaries.

nodes.label: Gate 2 — Feature Is Discoverable Without Docs
nodes.norm_label: gate 2 — feature is discoverable without docs

nodes.label: Zero-Blast-Radius Sandbox
nodes.rationale: Provides a safe target repository for evaluating AI coding agents without risking real production code or infrastructure.

nodes.label: Byte-Stable Regression Contract
nodes.rationale: The frozen stdout string acts as a language-agnostic correctness signal that makes regression checking deterministic and reproducible.

nodes.label: Zero External Dependencies
nodes.rationale: Keeping dependencies absent preserves the zero-blast-radius guarantee and keeps npm install as a no-op.

nodes.label: Governance Layer
nodes.rationale: The actual product is the governance layer rather than the runtime placeholder, so repository safety and rule compliance are first-class design goals.

nodes.label: ES5-Only JavaScript
nodes.rationale: The stack is deliberately locked to ES5 syntax to keep the benchmark minimal, reviewable, and free of transpilation or modern language ambiguity.
